actions.printNoPrinter: No printer — pick a sticker sheet instead

printer.connectSerialHint: For OS-paired Bluetooth SPP printers (e.g. QL-820NWB)

unsupportedBrowser.title: Connecting a printer needs Chrome, Edge, or Opera.
unsupportedBrowser.body: You can keep designing here — printing needs a different browser.

perBrowser.firefox: WebSerial is shipping in Firefox Nightly. Stable Firefox can design but can't print to USB or serial printers yet — Chrome, Edge, or Opera work today.
perBrowser.safari: Safari and iOS browsers don't support web printer connections. Use Chrome, Edge, or Opera on a desktop or Android.
perBrowser.other: Use Chrome, Edge, or Opera to connect a printer.

unsupportedBrowser.whyExplanation: Connecting a printer from a website uses two web APIs — WebUSB and WebSerial — that ship in Chromium-based browsers. Firefox is starting to support WebSerial. Safari has chosen not to ship them. The editor itself works in any browser; only the printer-connection step needs Chromium.

barcode.noPlaceholders: Add a {token} to any field first, or import a CSV.

hint.code128a: Uppercase letters, digits and symbols (no lowercase). Example: HELLO 001
hint.code39: Letters, digits and a few symbols. We'll uppercase. Example: BURNMARK 01
hint.code93: Letters, digits and a few symbols. Example: BURNMARK 01
hint.codabar: Digits and a few symbols. Example: 12-34-56
hint.ean13: 12 digits (we'll add the checksum) or 13 digits with checksum.
hint.ean8: 7 digits (we'll add the checksum) or 8 digits with checksum.
hint.upca: 11 digits (we'll add the checksum) or 12 digits with checksum.
hint.itf14: 13 digits (we'll add the checksum) or 14 digits with checksum.
hint.hibccode128: HIBC LIC over Code 128. Letters, digits, symbols.
hint.microqr: Up to ~35 numeric / ~21 alpha / ~15 byte chars.
hint.datamatrix: Any text. Square or rectangular variant.
hint.maxicode: Up to ~93 chars. Modes 2/3 expect a postal payload.
hint.dotcode: Any text. Often GS1 AI syntax.
hint.gs1_cc: GS1 Composite Component with parenthesised AIs.
hint.royalmail: Letters and digits — we'll uppercase. RM4SCC.
hint.kix: Letters and digits — we'll uppercase. Dutch KIX.
hint.japanpost: Letters, digits and hyphen. Japan Post Customer Barcode.

validation.empty: Type something to render a barcode.
validation.placeholderBypass: Will be validated against the actual data when printed.
validation.checksumInvalid: {count} digits — checksum doesn't match. Drop the last digit and we'll add it for you.
validation.badAlphabet: Some characters aren't allowed for this format.
validation.evenDigitsRequired: Needs an even number of digits — got {got}.
validation.outOfRange: Value must be between {min} and {max} — got {got}.
validation.gs1UnknownAi: Unknown AI {ai} — this might not scan reliably.
validation.gs1MalformedAi: Couldn't read the AI structure. Use (AI)data, e.g. (01)12345678901234.
validation.capacityWarning: Long content — may need a higher version or lower error correction.

about.title: About burnmark
about.intro: burnmark is a free, open-source label designer that runs entirely in your browser. No account, no server, no install.
about.nameBody: Thermal printers work by burning tiny marks onto heat-sensitive paper. Each dot on your label is literally a burn mark — a precise point of heat from the print head. We thought that was a pretty cool name for a label app.
about.originBody: It started with a messy workshop and a Dymo LabelManager that refused to cooperate on Linux. Instead of organising the workshop, we ended up reverse-engineering USB printer protocols and accidentally building an entire label printing ecosystem. The workshop is still not fully organised.
about.projectBody: burnmark is part of the thermal-label ecosystem — open-source drivers for Dymo and Brother label printers, a headless design engine, and this web app. Everything is MIT-licensed.
about.supportBody: burnmark is built and maintained by Mannes Brak in the Netherlands. If you find it useful, consider supporting the project:

help.title: Help
help.restartTourHint: Re-runs the welcome tour from step 1.
help.shortcutsHint: Speed up everything with the keyboard.
help.compatibilityHint: Which printers work, and which browsers they need.
help.docsHint: How-tos and protocol notes at thermal-label.github.io.
help.compatIntro: burnmark works best in Chrome or Edge.
help.compatChrome: Chrome (desktop and Android): full support — WebUSB, Web Serial, PWA.
help.compatFirefox: Firefox: design and export only — no printing (WebUSB not supported).
help.compatSafari: Safari: design and export only — no printing.
help.compatBrother: Brother QL series (QL-800, QL-810W, QL-820NWB, QL-1100, QL-1110NWB)
help.compatConnectBody: USB cable or Bluetooth (Brother QL-820NWB via Web Serial). Android users: a USB-C OTG adapter (€5) connects directly.

tour.completedToast: You're ready! Need help later? Check the help menu.
tour.step1Body: This is a sample — click any element to edit, or start fresh.
tour.step2Body: Text, images, barcodes, shapes — pick one and click the label.
tour.step3Title: Edit objects in the side panel
tour.step3Body: Click any object in the list to edit its properties.
tour.step4Body: Connect your printer and hit print. That's it!

howStored.heading: How burnmark stores your data
howStored.body1: Everything you make in burnmark stays on this device. No account, no server, no analytics — your designs and data never leave your browser.
howStored.body2: If you want to remove burnmark's data from this device, your browser's site-data controls do it cleanly.

yourData.heading: What you have on this device

encryption.heading: Encryption
encryption.offDescription: Your data is currently stored in plain form. Anyone with access to this browser profile can read your designs and datasets.
encryption.onDescription: Encryption is on. Your data is unreadable on disk without your password.
encryption.whyBody: If this browser is shared — a family or office machine — encryption keeps your saved labels and datasets unreadable to anyone who doesn't know your password. It does not protect against malware on the same device, and it does not protect data while the app is unlocked.

reset.heading: Reset
reset.body: Wipe every saved label, dataset, image, and preference from this device. This cannot be undone.

setup.title: Encrypt your local data
setup.warningRecoveryHeading: If you forget this password, your data is gone
setup.warningRecovery: There is no recovery, no hint, no reset link. We do not have a copy of your password — burnmark runs entirely on your device.
setup.warningScope: Encryption keeps your data unreadable when someone else uses this browser. It does not protect against malware on this device or against anyone using the app while it is unlocked.
setup.noteUnsaved: Your current design will save normally afterwards — you do not need to save before encrypting.
setup.lengthHint: Longer passwords are stronger. A short phrase you'll remember beats a complex word you might forget.
setup.acknowledge: I understand my data is gone if I lose this password.
setup.errorTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
setup.errorGeneric: Something went wrong while encrypting. Please try again.

disable.title: Turn off encryption
disable.body: Your data will be returned to plaintext on this device. You'll need to enter your current password to confirm.

reset.title: Reset all data
reset.body: Every saved label, dataset, image, and preference on this device will be permanently removed. The app will reload to a clean state.
reset.warning: This cannot be undone. There is no backup.

unlock.error: Couldn't unlock with this passkey. Try your password.

nudge.title: Unlock burnmark faster
nudge.body: Use your device's biometric or hardware key to unlock instead of typing your password every time. Your password still works as a backup.

privacy.heading: Devices that can unlock burnmark
privacy.unsupportedNote: This browser doesn't support adding passkeys for unlock.
privacy.synced: Passkeys synced via iCloud or Google Password Manager carry across the devices linked to that account. Use a hardware key if you want one device only.
privacy.confirmRemove: Remove the passkey? You'll need your password to unlock from now on. You may also want to remove burnmark from your device's saved passkeys.
privacy.disableNote: Your passkey for burnmark will stop working. You may also want to remove burnmark from your device's saved passkeys.

errors.prfNotSupported: This device's authenticator can't be used here. Try a different one or use your password.
errors.failed: Couldn't add the passkey. Please try again.
